prose:
The Fukui indices for pyridine have been calculated using density functional theory (DFT) with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard'). The computation was performed in the gas phase (explicitly no solvent), with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. Mulliken partial charges were used. The geometry corresponds to the structure provided in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz.  

Method provenance: **Fukui Reactivity Indices (Mulliken)** — DFT, **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** (PySCF, density-fit enabled), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: input as provided; single point, no relaxation. 

Key results: The most electrophilic atom in pyridine is the nitrogen (index 0-based or label 1-based), with a Fukui-plus index of **0.48423**. The nitrogen exhibits the highest sensitivity to electrophilic attack at the selected level of theory.
